title: Trace.WriteLineIf should be avoided with TraceSwitch levels
description: The Trace.WriteLineIf Method from the System.Diagnostic.Trace facility writes a trace if the condition passed as the first parameter is true. TraceSwitch allows trace control via bool properties for each relevant TraceLevel, such as TraceSwitch.TraceError. Using Trace.WriteLineIf with such properties should be avoided since it can lead to misinterpretation and produce confusion.
recommendation: Refactor to eliminate the flagged pattern.

noncompliant:
var traceSwitch = new TraceSwitch("mySwitch", "Description");
Trace.WriteLineIf(traceSwitch.TraceError, "Error message"); // Noncompliant

checks.message: Trace.WriteLineIf with TraceSwitch levels should be avoided. Use Trace.TraceError, Trace.TraceWarning, or Trace.TraceInformation instead.

never_trigger_on: Trace.WriteLineIf with boolean conditions (not TraceSwitch levels)
logic.reasoning: Using Trace.WriteLineIf with TraceSwitch level properties (TraceError, TraceWarning, etc.) can lead to misinterpretation and confusion. The preferred approach is to use the built-in Trace.TraceError, Trace.TraceWarning, and Trace.TraceInformation methods which provide clearer semantics.
